programs.name: MITES Summer
programs.subject: STEM (math, science, engineering)
programs.cost: Free (all program costs covered; students pay only transportation to MIT)
programs.program_dates: About six weeks, late June to early August
programs.description: A free six-week residential STEM program at MIT for high school juniors, with coursework in math, life sciences, physics, humanities, and a project-based elective. Students apply online during the fall of their junior year; the program actively encourages applications from underrepresented and underserved students.

application_requirements.label: Create or resume the online MITES application
application_requirements.details: MITES Summer and MITES Semester share one application; applicants can choose one or both national programs.

application_requirements.label: Enter academic information and upload an unofficial transcript
application_requirements.details: The transcript should include GPA and completed courses from 9th and 10th grades plus first-quarter 11th-grade courses if available.

application_requirements.label: Complete the six short-answer responses
application_requirements.details: Most short responses have a maximum of 300 words; one MITES-fit prompt is listed as 400 words max.

application_requirements.label: Report extracurricular activities
application_requirements.details: MITES uses this section to understand applicants' interests and passions outside the classroom.

application_requirements.label: Send three recommendation requests
application_requirements.details: MITES requires one math/science teacher, one humanities teacher, and one school counselor or administrator recommendation through the online portal.

application_requirements.label: Submit standardized test scores if available
application_requirements.details: Test scores are not required, but MITES says applicants are strongly encouraged to submit at least one available standardized-test score.

verification.notes: Official MITES Summer and MITES application pages: entirely free except transportation, residential at MIT, six weeks late June to early August, eligible to high school juniors who are U.S. citizens or permanent residents. Application checklist source confirms online application, transcript, short answers, activities, recommendations, and optional test scores. A dated upcoming-cycle deadline is not published, so deadline remains VERIFY.

programs.name: Summer Science Program (SSP)
programs.subject: Hands-on science research
programs.description: A five-week residential research experience for rising high school seniors, with team-based research in astrophysics, biochemistry, bacterial genomics, or synthetic chemistry across multiple host college campuses. Need-based financial aid is offered.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online SSP application
application_requirements.details: The 2026 process used separate international and domestic deadlines; applications are submitted through SSP's application portal.

application_requirements.label: Choose one research project
application_requirements.details: Applicants must choose one project when registering and may not apply to more than one project after the deadline.

application_requirements.label: Confirm required coursework by project
application_requirements.details: Relevant coursework must be taken for credit and a grade and completed by June; requirements vary by research area.

application_requirements.label: Request at least one science or math educator recommendation
application_requirements.details: SSP requires one recommendation from a math or science educator; a second recommendation is not required but is highly preferred.

application_requirements.label: Track transcript submission in the portal
application_requirements.details: SSP's FAQ tells applicants to use the status page to confirm required parts and references transcript-page errors for incomplete applications.

application_requirements.label: Request financial aid after admission if needed
application_requirements.details: Financial aid is requested after admission decisions; SSP says need is not considered in admissions.

verification.notes: Official SSP pages: five-week residential research program for current high-school juniors/rising seniors; applications define domestic and international applicants, so citizenship is open. Fees page confirms tuition-based cost with need-based aid and potential stipends. The 2026 deadlines are published but the next cycle is not, so deadline remains VERIFY.

programs.name: PROMYS (Program in Mathematics for Young Scientists)
programs.subject: Mathematics
programs.cost: Tuition-based; need-based aid available up to full cost
programs.description: An intensive mathematics program at Boston University for strongly motivated high school students from the U.S. and around the world, focused on deep exploratory problem-solving. Need-based financial aid is available up to the full cost of participation.

application_requirements.label: Upload problem-set solutions as one PDF
application_requirements.details: PROMYS asks applicants to submit solutions to a challenging problem set; handwritten solutions must be scanned legibly and converted to PDF.

application_requirements.label: Upload a school transcript or report card
application_requirements.details: PROMYS accepts an unofficial copy; homeschooled students should upload a description of courses and studies undertaken.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online application form and short answers
application_requirements.details: The form asks for personal short answers and summaries of problem-set solutions in text boxes.

application_requirements.label: Request a mathematics teacher recommendation
application_requirements.details: Applicants enter the recommender's name and email in Submittable; PROMYS says the teacher form must be received for consideration.

application_requirements.label: Have a parent or guardian complete the financial aid form if applicable
application_requirements.details: Applicants seeking financial aid trigger a parent/guardian form through Submittable; PROMYS lists a separate 2026 financial-aid deadline.

verification.notes: Official PROMYS student/application pages: six-week residential mathematics program at Boston University for high-school students from the U.S. and around the world; 2026 dates/deadline, eligibility, cost, financial aid, and application components are published. Upcoming-cycle deadline remains VERIFY; 2026-02-27 is stored as the prior-cycle estimate.

programs.name: Telluride Association Summer Seminar (TASS)
programs.subject: Humanities and social sciences
programs.cost: Free; Telluride covers tuition, books, room and board, field trips, and facilities fees
programs.description: A free residential seminar for high school sophomores and juniors centered on college-level humanities and social-science study, democratic community, writing, public speaking, and leadership. TASS welcomes U.S. and international students, with extra age/grade nuance for students studying outside the U.S.

application_requirements.label: Submit the TASS online application
application_requirements.details: The official 2026 page lists the application opening and closing dates; future-cycle dates should be checked on the sponsor page.

application_requirements.label: Choose one TASS area of study
application_requirements.details: Applicants may apply to only one of the two TASS areas: Critical Black Studies or Anti-Oppressive Studies.

verification.notes: Official TASS page confirms free cost coverage, residential 2026 dates, sophomore/junior eligibility, U.S. and international eligibility, program areas, and the 2026 application timeline. Upcoming-cycle deadline remains VERIFY; 2025-12-03 is stored as the 2026-cycle deadline.

programs.name: Anson L. Clark Scholars Program
programs.subject: Research across STEM and other faculty areas
programs.cost: No program participation fee; room and board provided; $750 stipend after successful research report
programs.description: An intensive seven-week Texas Tech research program for highly qualified rising high school seniors and recent high school graduates. Scholars conduct hands-on research with faculty and receive campus room, board, programming, and a stipend after completing a successful research report.

application_requirements.label: Create an account and complete the online application
application_requirements.details: Applicants create an account from the Clark Scholars application site, save each section, and submit by the published deadline.

application_requirements.label: Complete the required short essays
application_requirements.details: Texas Tech says short essays are required and publishes prompts/limits for applicants to prepare in advance.

application_requirements.label: Upload transcript and standardized-test score report
application_requirements.details: Unofficial transcripts and score reports are accepted; at least a PSAT or PACT score is required if SAT/ACT scores are unavailable.

application_requirements.label: Request three recommendations
application_requirements.details: Three recommendations are required; at least two must be from teachers and all are completed through an online questionnaire.

application_requirements.label: List top five activities and accomplishments
application_requirements.details: The program asks applicants to list their five most important involvement activities or accomplishments; extra activity lists/resumes are not accepted.

verification.notes: Official Texas Tech Clark pages confirm seven-week dates, twelve-student scale, eligibility, no participation fees, room/board, $750 stipend, U.S./PR citizenship, application deadline, and components. Upcoming-cycle deadline remains VERIFY; 2026-02-16 is stored as the prior-cycle estimate.

programs.name: Stanford University Mathematics Camp (SUMaC)
programs.subject: Advanced mathematics
programs.cost: Tuition-based: 2026 online $3,750; residential $8,950; need-based aid available
programs.program_dates: 2026 online sessions: June 15 to July 3 or July 6 to July 24; residential: June 21 to July 17
programs.description: A selective Stanford mathematics program for students in grades 10 and 11 with strong interest in advanced math, proof-writing, algebra, number theory, or topology. SUMaC offered both online and residential options for 2026 and considers students from around the world.

application_requirements.label: Create a SUMaC application account
application_requirements.details: Applicants use a SUMaC account; parents or guardians are required to submit the application for minor students.

application_requirements.label: Rank SUMaC course preferences
application_requirements.details: Students rank the two available courses, Program I and Program II, in the application.

application_requirements.label: Upload academic records
application_requirements.details: SUMaC requires unofficial transcripts for schools or academic programs where the applicant earned grades over the listed period.

application_requirements.label: Request one math teacher recommendation
application_requirements.details: SUMaC requires one online recommendation form from a math teacher.

application_requirements.label: Complete the SUMaC admission exam
application_requirements.details: The admission exam is a set of challenging proof-based math problems completed at home before the application deadline.

application_requirements.label: Submit the optional video essay if useful
application_requirements.details: The video essay is optional; Stanford strongly encourages it for students whose school language of instruction is not English.

verification.notes: Official Stanford SUMaC pages confirm grades 10-11 eligibility, online/residential 2026 options and dates, tuition, financial aid, application deadline, records, recommendation, exam, and optional video essay. Citizenship has no restriction stated beyond global admission language, so it is marked any.

programs.name: Garcia Summer Research Program
programs.subject: Materials science and polymer research
programs.cost: Paid: 2026 laboratory usage fee $4,000; room, board, and meal plan not included
programs.selectivity: Competitive research program (admit rate not published)
programs.description: A seven-week in-person research program at Stony Brook's Garcia Center where high school students work in laboratories on original research projects. International students are eligible only if they already have documents allowing legal presence in the U.S. for the program period; the app cannot verify that condition yet.

application_requirements.label: Submit application form Parts 1 and 2
application_requirements.details: The 2026 Garcia application page lists Part 1 and Part 2 of the application form as required.

application_requirements.label: Have your school submit the transcript
application_requirements.details: The transcript must be submitted by school staff or via Parchment; Garcia says self-submitted transcripts are not accepted.

application_requirements.label: Request three letters of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Three letters are required, with at least one from a science teacher; letters must be submitted by the writer through the portal.

application_requirements.label: Pay the non-refundable registration fee
application_requirements.details: The 2026 application page lists a non-refundable $50 registration fee payable by check or wire transfer.

verification.notes: Official Garcia pages confirm 2026 dates, high-school age/GPA/course/test expectations, international conditional eligibility, application parts, transcript, recommendations, fee, in-person/commuter nature, no aid, and lab/housing costs. Deadline remains VERIFY because a specific deadline was not published on the fetched page.

programs.name: Simons Summer Research Program
programs.subject: Science, math, and engineering research
programs.cost: No tuition; participants cover transportation and dining or residential costs if dorming

special_requirements.label: High school nomination required
special_requirements.details: Each high school may nominate no more than two eligible students before the student application.

programs.description: A national Stony Brook research fellowship for academically talented 11th-grade students. Applicants must be nominated by their high school, be U.S. citizens or permanent residents, and select preferred faculty mentors or research areas.

application_requirements.label: Secure school nomination
application_requirements.details: Each high school may nominate no more than two eligible students; independently submitted applications can be disqualified.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online application
application_requirements.details: Applicants can begin after confirming nomination status; the 2026 application deadline was February 5.

application_requirements.label: Upload transcript or grade report
application_requirements.details: The online application asks students to upload an unofficial transcript or grade report as a PDF.

application_requirements.label: Answer brief application questions
application_requirements.details: Simons lists brief responses of 300 words or less and prohibits AI-generated or AI-rewritten application content.

application_requirements.label: Request two teacher recommendations
application_requirements.details: Two math/science-preferred teacher letters are required; prior research supervisor letters are encouraged if applicable.

application_requirements.label: Select top three mentor or research-area preferences
application_requirements.details: Applicants are required to select three preferred mentors or research areas in the online application.

verification.notes: Official Simons pages confirm junior-only, U.S./PR eligibility, nomination requirement, 2026 dates/deadlines, transcript, recommendations, brief questions, mentor choices, cost language, and about <5% admission. Deadline remains VERIFY for future cycle; 2026-02-05 is stored as prior-cycle estimate.

programs.name: California State Summer School for Mathematics and Science (COSMOS)
programs.subject: STEM
programs.cost: Paid: 2026 residential program cost $5,518 plus $46 application fee; financial assistance may be available
programs.selectivity: Competitive; each campus can accommodate about 160-200 participants
programs.program_dates: 2026 program dates: July 5 to July 31 or August 1, depending on campus
programs.description: A four-week UC residential STEM program for California students who will be in grades 9-12 in fall 2026. Students apply to a UC campus and cluster, live on campus, and study advanced STEM topics with faculty, researchers, and scientists.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online COSMOS application
application_requirements.details: The application includes one personal statement and campus/cluster choices.

application_requirements.label: Request teacher recommendation
application_requirements.details: One teacher recommendation is required and two are recommended.

application_requirements.label: Upload unofficial transcript or report-card materials
application_requirements.details: Unofficial transcripts are uploaded before submission; 8th and 9th graders must also submit prior-grade report cards.

application_requirements.label: Collect parent/guardian e-signature and pay application fee
application_requirements.details: COSMOS requires parent/guardian signature acknowledgment and a non-refundable application fee by the deadline.

application_requirements.label: Submit financial-assistance materials if needed
application_requirements.details: Financial assistance is optional and reviewed separately from admission; complete documentation is required by the stated deadline.

verification.notes: Official COSMOS pages confirm California-only eligibility, rising grades 9-12, residential STEM program, UC campuses, 2026 dates/cost/deadline, application materials, and financial assistance. Citizenship is not published on the reviewed pages, so it remains VERIFY.

programs.name: RISE Internship/Practicum
programs.subject: Science and engineering research
programs.cost: Paid: 2026 tuition $5,930 plus fees; housing and dining optional; limited need-based aid available
programs.selectivity: Competitive research program (admit rate not published)
programs.program_dates: 2026 residential dates: June 28 to August 7; commuter dates: June 29 to August 7
programs.description: A six-week Boston University research program for domestic students entering senior year. RISE offers an Internship track with faculty research and a Practicum track with structured group research, with residential and commuter options.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online RISE application
application_requirements.details: The application must be submitted by the prospective student and can be edited before submission.

application_requirements.label: Upload high-school transcript
application_requirements.details: BU requires a full high-school transcript beginning in 9th grade and including fall 2025 grades for the 2026 application.

application_requirements.label: Complete RISE essays
application_requirements.details: RISE requires three essays; Internship applicants also list three faculty of interest and explain research alignment.

application_requirements.label: Request one recommendation
application_requirements.details: The recommender should be a STEM teacher, guidance counselor, or research supervisor who has known the applicant for at least six months.

application_requirements.label: Upload standardized-test scores if choosing to submit
application_requirements.details: BU lists standardized tests as optional for summer 2026 applicants.

application_requirements.label: Upload financial-aid documents if applying for aid
application_requirements.details: Financial aid documents must be uploaded with the application; admission decisions are made without regard to aid requests.

verification.notes: Official BU RISE pages confirm junior/domestic eligibility, U.S./PR requirement, 2026 dates, residential/commuter options, tuition/fees/aid, tracks, application deadline, transcript, essays, recommendation, optional tests, and financial-aid materials.

programs.name: Summer Academy for Math and Science (SAMS)
programs.subject: STEM
programs.cost: Fully funded; no cost beyond travel to and from Pittsburgh
programs.location: Virtual Jumpstart plus Pittsburgh, PA (Carnegie Mellon University)
programs.program_dates: 2026 virtual Jumpstart: June 15-16; in-person program: June 20 to August 1
programs.description: A fully funded CMU program for 11th-grade students interested in STEM, combining a short virtual Jumpstart with six weeks in person at Carnegie Mellon. SAMS emphasizes math, science, research, writing, mentoring, and access for students underrepresented in STEM-related fields.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online SAMS application
application_requirements.details: CMU says the SAMS application includes more specific details, but applicants can expect an online application.

application_requirements.label: Upload unofficial high-school transcript
application_requirements.details: The application requirements list an unofficial high-school transcript.

application_requirements.label: Submit standardized test scores
application_requirements.details: CMU lists standardized test scores among expected SAMS application requirements.

application_requirements.label: Request two recommendation letters
application_requirements.details: Two letters are required; one must be from a current math instructor unless the applicant emails SAMS about that limitation.

application_requirements.label: Complete the SAMS essay prompts
application_requirements.details: Applicants complete one general CMU Pre-College essay and one SAMS-specific essay prompt.

application_requirements.label: Submit required financial documentation
application_requirements.details: SAMS says financial documentation is required for application submission because the program is fully funded and merit-based.

verification.notes: Official CMU SAMS page confirms fully funded cost, hybrid virtual/in-person structure, 2026 dates, resident-only in-person phase, grade/age/citizenship eligibility, financial documentation, application components, and essay prompts. Deadline remains VERIFY; 2026-02-01 is a prior-cycle estimate.

programs.name: Leadership Seminars
programs.subject: Leadership, global issues, business, law, and technology
programs.cost: Tuition, housing, and meals paid by Notre Dame; application, enrollment, and travel fees remain
programs.selectivity: Competitive; approximately 150 students admitted each year
programs.description: A competitive 10-day residential Notre Dame program for current high-school juniors entering senior year who show strong academic preparation and leadership. Courses span business, global issues, inequality, law, AI/data/technology, and financial literacy.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online Leadership Seminars application
application_requirements.details: Notre Dame lists the application opening and deadline dates for the 2026 cycle.

application_requirements.label: Provide counselor contact for transcript, counselor report, and school profile
application_requirements.details: Notre Dame sends an auto-email to the counselor; applicants are responsible for following up.

application_requirements.label: Request a high-school teacher recommendation
application_requirements.details: Applicants provide a recommending teacher's name and email; Notre Dame sends the teacher an auto-email.

application_requirements.label: Upload standardized-test scores if submitting
application_requirements.details: SAT, ACT, or PSAT scores are preferred but not required; international applicants outside English-speaking schools have an English-test requirement.

application_requirements.label: Pay the application fee
application_requirements.details: Notre Dame lists a $75 application fee for Leadership Seminars.

verification.notes: Official Notre Dame pages confirm 10-day residential dates, approximately 150 admits, tuition/housing/meals coverage, junior eligibility, top academic/leadership profile, application deadline, counselor report/transcript, teacher rec, optional tests, English test for some international applicants, and fees.

programs.name: Yale Young Global Scholars (YYGS)
programs.subject: Interdisciplinary global issues, STEM, law, economics, and technology
programs.cost: Paid: 2026 residential tuition $7,000; need-based financial aid available up to full tuition
programs.program_dates: 2026 residential sessions: June 21 to July 3, July 5 to July 17, or July 19 to July 31
programs.description: A two-week residential academic enrichment program at Yale for outstanding high school students from around the world. YYGS offers interdisciplinary sessions in science and technology, politics/law/economics, and global challenges, with need-based aid available to domestic and international students.

application_requirements.label: Complete and submit the YYGS online application
application_requirements.details: Applicants must submit the application from the Review page and then pay or resolve the fee in the Applicant Status Portal.

application_requirements.label: Enter activities list
application_requirements.details: YYGS lists an activities list as a required application component.

application_requirements.label: Complete essay and short-response questions
application_requirements.details: YYGS requires one 400-word essay, one 200-word response, and two 280-character fast-take responses.

application_requirements.label: Submit official school transcript
application_requirements.details: An official school transcript is listed among required YYGS components.

application_requirements.label: Request one YYGS recommender form
application_requirements.details: YYGS accepts only the online recommendation form, not written letters.

application_requirements.label: Complete financial-aid section if applying for aid
application_requirements.details: Need-based aid is included in the online application and can cover up to 100% of tuition for domestic and international students.

verification.notes: Official YYGS pages confirm residential 2026 sessions, global eligibility, sophomore/junior eligibility, all-country applications and aid, tuition, application deadlines, components, essays, transcript, recommender form, and financial-aid process. Upcoming-cycle deadline remains VERIFY; 2026-01-07 is stored as prior-cycle estimate.

programs.name: Research Science Institute (RSI)
programs.host: Center for Excellence in Education (CEE) at MIT
programs.subject: STEM research
programs.selectivity: Highly competitive (~100 students selected nationally and internationally)
programs.program_dates: About six weeks in summer (one week coursework plus five-week research internship)
programs.description: Cost-free summer science and engineering program at MIT combining intensive STEM coursework with a five-week research internship. High school juniors apply directly to CEE; seniors are not eligible.

application_requirements.label: Submit the RSI application to CEE
application_requirements.details: U.S. citizens and permanent residents apply directly to CEE; international applicants follow country-specific selection where available.

application_requirements.label: Complete essay responses on STEM goals
application_requirements.details: Essays should detail goals in science, technology, engineering, or mathematics.

application_requirements.label: Submit two teacher recommendations (max three)
application_requirements.details: Math/science teachers or research supervisors familiar with the applicant; research supervisors preferred for students with 4+ week lab experience.

application_requirements.label: Upload official transcript and standardized test scores
application_requirements.details: Include PSAT, SAT, ACT, and AP scores; PSAT strongly encouraged.

verification.notes: Official CEE RSI pages: free program at MIT; juniors only; U.S. citizens/PR apply to CEE; RSI 2026 applications closed; upcoming deadline not published.

programs.name: Bank of America Student Leaders
programs.subject: Leadership and community service
programs.cost: Paid six-week internship ($17/hour or local minimum wage, whichever is higher)
programs.selectivity: Competitive (300+ students; top 20% of recommended applicants)
programs.location: Eligible U.S. program markets (local nonprofit placement)
programs.program_dates: Six weeks mid-June through July 2026, including mandatory 3-day Leadership Summit (June 16–18, 2026)

special_requirements.label: Post-secondary credit-hour window
special_requirements.details: Applicants must have earned 12–18 post-secondary credit hours and achieved a high school diploma within the last 24 months.

programs.description: Paid six-week nonprofit internship plus an all-expenses-paid Leadership Summit for community-minded young adults authorized to work in the U.S. Program targets early college students with leadership experience, not high school juniors.

application_requirements.label: Apply through the CyberGrants Student Leaders portal
application_requirements.details: Apply at cybergrants.com/boa/slselection when the application opens.

application_requirements.label: Provide post-secondary transcript
application_requirements.details: Transcript must verify credit hours earned.

application_requirements.label: Obtain two recommendations after applying
application_requirements.details: Recommendations from college or high school officials; not from family or close friends.

verification.notes: Official BofA FAQ: 2026 cycle Feb 9–March 16; 12–18 credits; age 18+; paid internship and summit; 2026 deadline passed.

programs.name: American Legion Boys State
programs.subject: Civic leadership and government
programs.cost: Typically sponsored (Legion posts often cover participant costs; varies by state)
programs.selectivity: Selective (Legion post or school nomination; varies by department)
programs.location: Varies by state (49 Boys State programs)
programs.program_dates: Varies by state department (typically summer after junior year)

special_requirements.label: Legion post or school selection
special_requirements.details: Students are typically recommended by schools to local Legion posts, which select delegates; process varies by department.

programs.description: Selective week-long residential program where high school juniors operate mock city, county, and state governments. Founded in 1935; each state department runs its own dates, costs, and selection process.

application_requirements.label: Find your state Boys State program and contacts
application_requirements.details: Use register.legion.org/boysnation/stateeligibility for state links, dates, and application instructions.

application_requirements.label: Work with your school and sponsoring Legion post
application_requirements.details: Ideal path is school recommendation to a local post, which interviews and selects representatives.

verification.notes: Official Legion pages: juniors; state-specific dates/deadlines; post selection; programs in 49 states except Hawaii.

programs.name: ALA Girls State
programs.subject: Civic leadership and government
programs.cost: Typically sponsored (ALA units often cover participant costs; varies by state)
programs.selectivity: Selective (ALA unit recruitment; varies by state)
programs.location: Varies by state ALA Girls State program
programs.program_dates: Varies by state program (typically summer after junior year)

special_requirements.label: ALA unit or school recruitment
special_requirements.details: Local ALA units recruit and select delegates; contact your state program for dates and application process.

special_requirements.label: Women participants only
special_requirements.details: ALA Girls State is the Auxiliary's mock-government program for high school junior women; eligibility follows each state program's rules.

programs.description: Parallel to American Legion Boys State, ALA Girls State is a selective mock-government leadership program for high school junior women, operated at the state level by the American Legion Auxiliary.

application_requirements.label: Contact your state ALA Girls State program
application_requirements.details: Recruitment is managed by local ALA units and state programs; guidance counselors are a common contact point.

verification.notes: Legion about page confirms ALA Girls State as companion program; state-level operation; official ALA site for program outreach.

programs.name: NIH Summer Internship Program (SIP)
programs.subject: Biomedical research
programs.location: NIH campuses (Bethesda, MD and other sites)

special_requirements.label: Age and proximity rules for high school seniors
special_requirements.details: High school seniors must be 18 by Sept 30 of the internship year; 17-year-olds as of June 1 must live within 40 miles of an NIH campus.

programs.description: Summer biomedical research internships in the NIH Intramural Research Program for high school seniors, undergraduates, and graduate students. Apply through the NIH Application Center; investigators select candidates for lab placements.

application_requirements.label: Apply through the NIH Application Center
application_requirements.details: Complete profile, select Summer internships, pass eligibility screener, and submit application materials.

application_requirements.label: Submit personal statement
application_requirements.details: Describe research interests, experiences, and career goals.

application_requirements.label: Provide two references
application_requirements.details: Two references required through the application system.

verification.notes: Official NIH SIP page: U.S. citizen or PR; HS seniors and college students; 2026 closed Feb 18; program May 11–Aug 31, 2026.

programs.name: JAX Summer Student Program
programs.subject: Genetics and genomics research
programs.cost: $7,500 stipend plus room, board, and round-trip travel
programs.location: Bar Harbor, ME and Farmington, CT
programs.program_dates: 10 weeks: May 30 – August 7, 2026 (Bar Harbor) or May 30 – August 10, 2026 (Farmington)
programs.description: Ten-week paid research fellowship in genetics and genomics for high school seniors and undergraduates at Jackson Laboratory campuses. Students may not hold other jobs or take classes during the program.

application_requirements.label: Submit the online JAX SSP application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the JAX education application portal; save progress and submit before the deadline.

application_requirements.label: Complete all required application materials
application_requirements.details: See the admission page checklist for personal, academic, and essay materials required for review.

verification.notes: Official JAX admission page: $7,500 stipend plus room/board/travel; 2026 deadline Jan 26; HS seniors and undergraduates.

programs.name: MIT Women's Technology Program in Mechanical Engineering (WTP-ME)
programs.subject: Mechanical engineering

special_requirements.label: Women-focused engineering program
special_requirements.details: WTP-ME is a women-focused collaborative community that also serves students underserved in engineering; all applicants are reviewed equitably per official MIT WTP pages.

programs.description: Four-week residential introduction to mechanical engineering at MIT for rising high school seniors with strong math and science records and limited prior engineering exposure. WTP-ME emphasizes students underserved in engineering.

application_requirements.label: Complete the WTP online application
application_requirements.details: Apply through web.mit.edu/wtp/application.html when the cycle is open.

verification.notes: Official WTP-ME page: 20 students; juniors; four weeks; 2026 applications closed; program cost not confirmed on pages checked.

programs.name: NASA SEES High School Intern Program
programs.host: University of Texas Center for Space Research (NASA Science Activation)
programs.subject: Earth and space science research
programs.cost: No tuition; housing/meals provided for selected on-site interns; limited travel scholarships may be available
programs.selectivity: Nationally competitive (174 students selected for 2025 cohort per NASA Science)
programs.location: Virtual plus optional on-site at UT Austin, TX
programs.program_dates: June–July 2026: virtual work June–July 3; on-site July 5–18, 2026; symposium July 20–21, 2026
programs.description: Nationally competitive NASA-funded STEM internship where high school students analyze NASA mission data with mentors across aerospace, Earth observation, and planetary science. Hybrid format with distance-learning modules and optional residential week at UT Austin.

application_requirements.label: Apply at spacegrant.net/apps/sees
application_requirements.details: Submit application, essay questions, introduction video, and request recommendation forms by the published deadline.

application_requirements.label: Ensure recommendation form is submitted
application_requirements.details: Recommendation forms due March 1 for the 2026 cycle (one week after application deadline).

verification.notes: Official CSR SEES page: Feb 22, 2026 deadline passed; hybrid virtual/on-site; 2026 dates published; continuation contingent on funding.

programs.name: Stanford Institutes of Medicine Summer Research Program (SIMR)
programs.subject: Biomedical research
programs.cost: No program tuition ($50 application fee; fee waivers available)
programs.selectivity: Highly competitive (selection favors Bay Area students per official FAQ)

special_requirements.label: U.S. residency and age requirements
special_requirements.details: Must live in and attend high school in the U.S.; be 16+ by program start; U.S. citizen or permanent resident.

programs.description: Eight-week mentored biomedical research internship at Stanford Medicine institutes for current high school juniors and seniors. Students must be able to start on the first program day; Bay Area students are strongly favored.

application_requirements.label: Submit the SIMR SlideRoom application
application_requirements.details: Upload transcripts, test scores, and complete all forms; pay $50 fee or request a fee waiver.

application_requirements.label: Submit teacher recommendation via SlideRoom
application_requirements.details: Recommendation grace period extends one week after application deadline; student must follow up with recommender.

verification.notes: Official SIMR pages: juniors/seniors; U.S. citizen/PR; 16+; June 8–July 30, 2026; Feb 21, 2026 deadline passed; $50 application fee.

programs.name: MITES Semester
programs.subject: STEM and college preparation (virtual)
programs.program_dates: June through December (summer STEM courses plus fall college prep)
programs.description: Six-month national online STEM and college-preparation program (formerly MOSTEC) for high school juniors, with synchronous summer courses and fall webinars on college applications. Shares one application with MITES Summer.

application_requirements.label: Submit the shared MITES online application
application_requirements.details: Choose MITES Semester, MITES Summer, or both on one application during fall of junior year.

verification.notes: Official MITES Semester page: free; U.S. citizen or PR; juniors; June-December virtual program; shared application with MITES Summer.

programs.name: Brookhaven High School Research Program (HSRP)
programs.subject: STEM research
programs.cost: No program fee (commuter; housing not provided)
programs.description: Six-week commuter research program where students collaborate with Brookhaven scientific staff on hands-on STEM projects, weekly meetings, and a concluding poster session.

application_requirements.label: Complete the HSRP online application
application_requirements.details: Requires superintendent name/email, personal email, parent contact, and two STEM recommendation letters.

application_requirements.label: Submit two teacher or mentor recommendations
application_requirements.details: Complete applications include two recommendation letters; notifications may arrive as late as early June.

verification.notes: Official BNL page: age 16+; U.S. citizen or PR; recommended after 11th grade; commuter only; six-week full-time commitment.

programs.name: MIT Beaver Works Summer Institute (BWSI)
programs.host: MIT Lincoln Laboratory / MIT School of Engineering
programs.subject: STEM (robotics, AI, cybersecurity, and more)
programs.cost: Free online prerequisites; summer courses may require fee for families above income threshold

special_requirements.label: U.S. high school enrollment required
special_requirements.details: Must reside in and attend high school physically in the United States through the program; generally rising seniors selected for summer seats.

programs.description: Project-based STEM program hosted by MIT Lincoln Laboratory with free online prerequisite courses and competitive summer team-based courses in robotics, AI, cybersecurity, and related fields.

application_requirements.label: Complete the online prerequisite course for your chosen track
application_requirements.details: Summer enrollment is competitive and strongly based on final online course grades.

application_requirements.label: Submit the BWSI summer program application
application_requirements.details: Eligible students are in grade 11 or below; seniors and college students are not accepted.

verification.notes: Official BWSI FAQ: U.S. high school enrollment; grades 9-11; July 6-Aug 2 2026; in-person courses require adult housing; income-based fees possible.

programs.name: Wharton Leadership in the Business World (LBW)
programs.subject: Business and leadership
programs.description: Three-week residential introduction to undergraduate business education with team projects, simulations, and a capstone case competition. International applicants welcome.

application_requirements.label: Apply through Wharton Global Youth when applications open
application_requirements.details: Priority deadline January 28, 2026; final deadline March 18, 2026 for prior cycle.

verification.notes: Official Wharton Global Youth page: grade 11; 3.5 unweighted GPA; leadership experience; priority Jan 28 / final Mar 18 2026.

programs.name: Wharton Management & Technology Summer Institute (M&TSI)
programs.subject: Management and technology
programs.description: For-credit three-week summer institute for rising seniors exploring the integration of business and technology through Wharton and Penn Engineering coursework and a team prototype capstone.

application_requirements.label: Apply through Wharton Global Youth M&TSI portal
application_requirements.details: Priority deadline January 28, 2026; final deadline March 25, 2026 for 2026 cycle.

verification.notes: Official page: rising seniors; 75 seats; $9,000 fee; one college credit; priority Jan 28 / final Mar 25 2026.

programs.name: Stanford Pre-Collegiate Summer Institutes
programs.subject: Single-subject intensive study (STEM, humanities, business, and more)
programs.description: Online two-week intensive courses for grades 8-11 in subjects ranging from bioscience and engineering to creative writing and philosophy, with small classes and hands-on projects.

application_requirements.label: Apply through Stanford Pre-Collegiate Studies
application_requirements.details: Application deadline March 13, 2026 for listed 2026 sessions.

verification.notes: Official SPCS page: grades 8-11; online; Mar 13 2026 deadline; two summer 2026 sessions published.

programs.name: Iowa Young Writers' Studio (Summer Residential Program)
programs.subject: Creative writing
programs.description: Two-week immersive creative writing program at the University of Iowa for students in grades 10-12. Admissions decisions are based almost entirely on the writing sample.

application_requirements.label: Apply via Submittable during the two-week application window
application_requirements.details: 2026 window: Jan 19 12:00 a.m. CST through Feb 1 11:59 p.m. CST.

application_requirements.label: Upload genre-specific writing sample and statement of purpose
application_requirements.details: Separate Submittable forms for fiction/poetry/creative writing, TV writing, or playwriting.

application_requirements.label: Upload one letter of recommendation
application_requirements.details: Letter from an English teacher or writing mentor; applicant uploads the letter to Submittable.

verification.notes: Official UIowa IYWS page: grades 10-12; Jan 19-Feb 1 2026 application window; $2,500 fee; decisions April 3 2026.

programs.name: Fermilab TARGET Internship Program
programs.subject: Physics, engineering, and computer science research

special_requirements.label: Illinois high school enrollment required
special_requirements.details: Applicants must be enrolled in an Illinois high school; Fermilab high school programs do not provide housing.

programs.description: Six-week summer research internship at Fermilab for Illinois high school sophomores and juniors with strong interest in physics, math, computer science, or engineering.

application_requirements.label: Apply at internships.fnal.gov/target when open
application_requirements.details: Applications are due the winter before the summer program.

verification.notes: Official Fermilab TARGET page: IL high school sophomores/juniors; six weeks; ~15-25 cohort; no housing.

programs.name: Iowa Young Writers' Studio (6-Week Online Courses)
programs.subject: Creative writing (online)
programs.description: Asynchronous six-week online creative writing courses taught by graduates of the Iowa Writers' Workshop. Admissions emphasize enthusiasm and commitment more than polished publication-ready work.

application_requirements.label: Apply via iowayoungwritersstudio.submittable.com
application_requirements.details: Summer 2026 applications accepted March 1 through April 20, 2026.

verification.notes: Official UIowa pages: grades 9-12; Mar 1-Apr 20 2026 window; $475 fee; June 14-July 26 2026 session dates.

programs.name: Wharton Moneyball Academy
programs.subject: Sports analytics and data science
programs.program_dates: Three weeks in summer (dates vary by year)
programs.description: Wharton Global Youth residential academy introducing statistics, data science, and analytics through sports contexts for academically strong high school students.

application_requirements.label: Apply through Wharton Global Youth when the cycle opens
application_requirements.details: Applications open in November per Wharton Global Youth program pages.

verification.notes: Official Wharton Global Youth Moneyball Academy page: residential analytics program; shares Global Youth application timeline with other on-campus programs.

programs.name: Rockefeller University SSRP
programs.subject: Biomedical research
programs.description: Free seven-week biomedical research program in New York City for high school juniors and seniors age 16 or older. Jan 2, 2026 deadline has passed; next cycle estimated January 2, 2027.

application_requirements.label: Submit the SSRP online application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the Rockefeller SSRP portal when the cycle opens.

verification.notes: Official Rockefeller SSRP page: free 7-week NYC program, juniors/seniors 16+, Jan 2 2026 deadline passed.

programs.name: CSHL Partners for the Future
programs.subject: Biomedical research

special_requirements.label: Science chair nomination required
special_requirements.details: Long Island high school seniors must be nominated by their school's science department chair before applying.

programs.description: Long Island high school seniors conduct mentored research at Cold Spring Harbor Laboratory from September through March after nomination by their school's science department chair.

application_requirements.label: Secure science department chair nomination
application_requirements.details: The school's science chair must nominate the student before the student completes the application.

application_requirements.label: Complete the student application after nomination
application_requirements.details: Nominated seniors submit the Partners for the Future application through CSHL.

verification.notes: Official CSHL page: Long Island seniors, school science chair nomination, Sep–Mar research year.

programs.name: MDI Biological Laboratory High School Summer Fellowship
programs.subject: Biomedical and environmental research
programs.description: Seven-week paid research fellowship for up to 10 high school students. Commuters must be age 16+; residential participants must be age 18+.

verification.notes: Official MDIBL FAQ: up to 10 fellows, 7 weeks, stipend, age 16 commuter/18 resident.

programs.name: Johns Hopkins CTY On-Campus Summer Programs

programs.description: Day and residential summer courses for grades 2–12 at multiple campuses. Many courses require qualifying test scores or CTY eligibility assessments.

application_requirements.label: Establish CTY eligibility
application_requirements.details: Submit qualifying test scores or complete CTY's eligibility process before enrolling in score-gated courses.

application_requirements.label: Register for on-campus summer courses
application_requirements.details: Enroll in chosen day or residential courses through the CTY registration portal.

programs.name: Northwestern CTD Summer Programs

programs.description: PreK–12 summer enrichment with day, residential, and online options through Northwestern CTD. Course eligibility varies by grade and assessment pathway.

application_requirements.label: Apply or register through CTD
application_requirements.details: Submit the CTD summer application or registration for chosen day, residential, or online programs.

programs.name: Georgia Tech CEISMC Summer PEAKS

programs.name: Georgia Tech Summer Institute (GTSI)
programs.subject: STEM residential institute

special_requirements.label: School metrics and nomination-style selection
special_requirements.details: Rising 11th graders must meet free/reduced lunch, top 10% class rank, and precalculus readiness criteria through school-based selection.

application_requirements.label: Apply through the school-based selection process
application_requirements.details: Students are identified through school metrics including lunch status, class rank, and math readiness.

application_requirements.label: Submit the official program application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the host's official summer program page when the cycle opens.

verification.notes: Official Harvard Pre-College page: 2 weeks, $6,100, rising juniors/seniors; early deadline Jan 7 / regular Feb 11 2026 — est. 2027-01-07 next early deadline.

programs.name: Stanford Summer Session for High School Students

programs.description: Stanford Summer Session allows qualified high school students in grades 10–12 to take Stanford courses for credit alongside college students. Participants must be ages 16–19 by the start of the program.

application_requirements.label: Submit the official program application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the host's official summer program page when the cycle opens.

verification.notes: Official Stanford Summer Session high school page: grades 10–12, 8 weeks Jun 20–Aug 16 2026, ages 16–19.

programs.name: MDIBL Biomedical Bootcamp

programs.description: One-week commuter biomedical research bootcamp for high school students entering junior or senior year (or graduating June 2026). Students must be at least 16 and commute daily to the MDIBL campus.

application_requirements.details: Apply through the host's official summer program page when the cycle opens.

programs.name: UChicago Research in the Biological Sciences (RIBS)
programs.subject: Biological sciences laboratory research

special_requirements.label: Residential requirement for Chicago residents
special_requirements.details: All participants must live in campus housing; students who live in Chicago are not eligible to commute. Program is limited to grades 10–11.

programs.description: Four-week intensive residential practicum introducing molecular, microbiological, and cellular biology research techniques. High school biology required; advanced biology recommended. Chicago residents must live on campus — commuting is not permitted.

application_requirements.label: Submit the official program application
application_requirements.details: Apply through the host's official summer program page when the cycle opens.

verification.notes: Official UChicago RIBS course page: 4 weeks residential, grades 10–11, $15,200; application deadline Feb 11 2026 — est. 2027-02-11; Chicago residents cannot commute.
